title: NOC 74200 – Railway yard and track maintenance workers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 74200 professionals are the precision operators who manage the complex logistics of railway yards and ensure the structural integrity of Canada's vast rail network. As a TEER 4 occupation, this group is split into two critical functions: Yard Workers, who regulate train traffic and switching, and Track Maintenance Workers, who operate heavy machinery to lay and repair tracks. In 2026, as Canada accelerates the High-Frequency Rail (HFR) corridor and expands its "Green Freight" rail capacity, these workers are classified as Strategic Infrastructure Talent. For immigration, this role is a priority for the Express Entry Transport Category and the Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level4Major Group74 – Mail and message distribution, transport equipment operators and maintenanceSub-Major Group742 – Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related workersMinor Group7420 – Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related workersNOC Code74200

duties: Yard Traffic Regulation: (Yard Workers) Operate control panel switches from towers to set traffic signals and activate switches that regulate train movement.
Coupling & Switching: Operate car retarders, turntables, and track switches to align locomotives and cars for coupling, uncoupling, or servicing.
Heavy Equipment Operation: (Track Maintenance) Operate specialized machines such as sleeper sliders, rail lifters, drag drills, and tie-bar cranes to lay and repair tracks.
Ballast Management: Spread, level, and tamp ballast around ties and rails to ensure stable and safe track alignment.
Inspection & Patrol: Patrol assigned track sections to identify and report damaged rails, broken switches, or drainage issues.
Maintenance: Perform minor repairs and cleaning on track-laying machinery; clear snow and ice from tracks and switches using blowers and heaters.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Experience: Experience as a railway labourer (NOC 75211) is typically required before progressing to these specialized roles.

Certifications:Yard Workers: Must obtain a Canadian Rail Operating Rules (CROR) certificate.Track Maintenance: Internal employer certification for operating specific heavy track machinery is required.
Yard Workers: Must obtain a Canadian Rail Operating Rules (CROR) certificate.
Track Maintenance: Internal employer certification for operating specific heavy track machinery is required.
Safety: All workers must pass a pre-employment medical exam and safety-sensitive drug/alcohol testing.
Physical stamina for outdoor work in extreme weather and the ability to work rotating shifts (nights/weekends) are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: Experienced Yard Tower Operators and Heavy Machine Operators in the federal rail sector often earn annual salaries between $75,000 and $95,000 inclusive of overtime and shift premiums.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the sector will face a significant wave of retirements over the next decade. As Canada integrates high-frequency rail and shifts more freight from road to rail for carbon reduction, the demand for technicians capable of maintaining advanced high-speed tracks ensures long-term job security through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for railway yard and track workers in 2026 is Strong, particularly in the Prairies and the Windsor-Quebec City corridor. In Saskatchewan and Alberta, the demand is driven by the export of potash and agricultural products. In Ontario, the massive $12-billion investment in rail electrification has created a surge in hiring for maintenance crews. According to 2026 Job Bank data, the industry is seeing a 12% increase in demand for "Maintenance of Way" employees to support 24/7 logistics operations.
The Rail Modernization Era: Work with cutting-edge automated switching systems and advanced track-laying technology (e.g., high-speed ballast regulators).
Exceptional Public/Private Stability: Secure roles with major national carriers like CN Rail and CPKC, or with regional transit authorities like Metrolinx.
Unionized Protection: Over 85% of workers in this NOC benefit from strong union representation, offering superior job security and comprehensive pension plans.
Strategic PR Pathways: This role is a core part of the 2026 Express Entry Category-Based Selection for "Transport Occupations," often leading to faster PR invitations.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Highly targeted under the Transport Occupations category in 2026.
Saskatchewan SINP (Hard-to-Fill Skills): A high-priority stream for track workers with a valid job offer in the province.
New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A dedicated stream for 33,000 workers already in Canada, targeting essential transport and infrastructure roles.
OINP Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): Frequently targets railway maintenance workers for regions outside the Greater Toronto Area (GTA).
